mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 147,241k as of 2017-11-01 indicates sustained employment growth
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months do not produce significantly higher 20-day forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate exceeding 2.0% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates on future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.16% as of 2017-11-01 remains below tightening threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising policy rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining more than 10% month-over-month signals weakening residential demand and forward-looking economic contraction, which reduces equity valuations through lower expected corporate earnings over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,290k as of 2017-10-01 shows stable housing construction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite housing decline, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerating above 0.5% month-over-month combined with rising payroll employment signals supply-side capacity expansion and productivity gains, driving positive equity returns over 20 trading days through improved profit margins.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth > 0.5% AND PAYEMS growth > 100k
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.40 and PAYEMS at 147,241k as of 2017-11-01
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor expansion in mid-cycle expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary policy constraint that compresses equity valuations, producing negative 60-day forward returns as real discount rates rise.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 247.59 and FEDFUNDS at 1.16% as of 2017-11-01
failure_condition: Interaction term Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and forward earnings contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations compress in response to reduced consumption expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 492,717M as of 2017-11-01 shows robust retail activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of months with declining RSAFS, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period signals labor market deterioration and forward earnings risk, producing negative 60-day equity returns as growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% as of 2017-11-01 indicates tight labor market
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment rise, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust aggregate demand expansion that supports equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 2.5% annualized AND RSAFS growth > 0.5% monthly
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17,169.73B and RSAFS at 492,717M as of 2017-11-01
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion in mid-to-late cycle
failure_condition: Interaction term Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 60% of triggered instances.
